# Pounce

> A free browser side-scrolling cat platformer. Pick from four cat palettes
> and bound through three hand-built levels (960 / 720 / 720 tiles wide) on
> the way to a cozy bed. Cat-food boxes power you up, magic fish unlock
> fishbone projectiles, dogs and crawling kids can be stomped — wasps you
> have to shoot or down-pounce. Vanilla HTML / CSS / JavaScript with no
> build step or install required; Web Audio synthesised soundtrack; global
> high-score leaderboard; keyboard, touch, and gamepad input; mid-run save.

Pounce is an open-source browser game in the same family as
[cat-ski](https://ski.mike-lee.me/) — same author, same vanilla stack, same
plain-prose voice. Four selectable cat palettes (black, tabby, calico,
orange) with vector cat sprites shared with cat-ski. Three hand-built
levels (MEADOW WALK, NIGHT GARDEN, HOMEWARD BOUND), each structured around
the four-act kishōtenketsu framework (intro → development → twist →
conclusion) and the six 2D-platformer level patterns (Guidance,
Foreshadowing, Safe Zone, Layering, Branching, Pace Breaking).

The cat starts small and weak. Hitting a `?`-style box pops a cat-food
can; eating it makes the cat big (one extra hit absorbed). Hitting a box
while big pops a magic fish; eating it grants the shooter state and the
ability to fire fishbone projectiles with the X key. Three obstacle
animals oppose: the dog (walking), the crawling small child (short, slow),
and the flying wasp — wasps cannot be stomped, only shot or down-pounced.
Down-pounce is a Mario-3-style ground-pound: in-air S or ↓ snaps the cat
into a fast straight-down slam that one-shots anything below, including
wasps, for a +200 score bonus.

Three difficulty modes (Easy / Normal / Hard) trade off lives, timer, and
enemy speed. A high-contrast mode is available via the C key for
accessibility. A mid-run snapshot auto-saves every two seconds — closing
the tab and coming back lets you resume from the title screen. Camera
look-ahead biases the view in the cat's facing direction; tutorial hints
fade in along the early stretch of level 1; and a settle-to-bed animation
plays when the cat reaches the goal.

Backed by Supabase for a global top-3 leaderboard, with a localStorage
cache so the strip paints instantly. Web Audio synthesised soundtrack
(chiptune I–V–vi–IV in C, 132 BPM) with a panic-mode tempo speedup at the
last 30 seconds; separate music and SFX toggles plus volume sliders.
Installable as a Progressive Web App with an offline service worker.
Keyboard, touch, and standard-layout gamepads all supported. Hosted on
Vercel, source on GitHub.

## Pages

- [Play Pounce](https://pounce.mike-lee.me/): The game itself. Loads in
  any modern browser. Keyboard, touch, and gamepad supported.
- [Building Pounce, a Browser Cat Platformer](https://pounce.mike-lee.me/readme.html):
  A walkthrough article covering the tile-based level architecture, the
  four-act kishōtenketsu pacing applied across three levels, the vector
  cat sprites shared with cat-ski, and the free toolchain used to ship it.
- [Privacy policy](https://pounce.mike-lee.me/privacy.html): Lists every
  localStorage key the game writes and what's sent to Supabase.

## About the project

- Author: Mike Lee (Whisker Leaks)
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mikelee89/
- Source code: https://github.com/ashtonmorrow/bros
- Sister projects: [cat-ski](https://ski.mike-lee.me/),
  [pear](https://pear.mike-lee.me/),
  [go](https://go.mike-lee.me/) — all by the same author at
  [mike-lee.me](https://mike-lee.me/)
- License: open source (see repository)
- Stack: vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript; HTML5 Canvas; Web Audio API;
  Supabase for the global leaderboard; no build step; no framework; no
  runtime dependencies beyond Google Fonts
- Hosting: Vercel, deployed automatically from the GitHub repository on push
- PWA: installable on iOS, Android, and desktop

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ashtonmorrow/bros): Issues, forks,
  and pull requests welcome.
- [Mike Lee on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mikelee89/): Project
  author contact.
